name: Nightshade
description: Nightshade (Solanum nigrum and related Solanum species), also called Black Nightshade or Common Nightshade, is a toxic plant for cats found in gardens, roadsides, and as a weed worldwide. Like all members of the nightshade family (Solanaceae), it contains steroidal glycoalkaloids — primarily solanine and solasonine — with the highest concentrations in unripe berries, leaves, and stems.
safetyNotes: Toxic compounds: solanine and solasonine (steroidal glycoalkaloids); unripe green berries and green plant parts most concentrated; ripe black berries slightly less toxic
Mechanism: glycoalkaloids inhibit acetylcholinesterase (disrupting nerve signalling) and disrupt cell membranes, causing GI and neurological effects
Symptoms: drooling, vomiting, diarrhea, abdominal pain, lethargy, weakness; severe ingestion may cause tremors, bradycardia, or CNS depression
Unripe berries are the greatest risk — they resemble edible berries and may attract curious cats
Contact your vet if your cat ingests any part of the plant, especially unripe berries

symptoms.name: Vomiting, diarrhea (sometimes bloody), and significant abdominal pain
symptoms.notes: Solanine interacts directly with the cell membranes, specifically causing destruction, irritation, and inflammation to the intestinal epithelial cells7. This leads to violent lower gastrointestinal problems and tense abdominal walls46. 2. Hypersalivation and Mucous Membrane Irritation

symptoms.name: Excessive drooling (hypersalivation), burning sensation, and coughing
symptoms.notes: This is caused by the direct irritating effect of the plant material on the mucous membranes, as well as the overstimulation of the parasympathetic nervous system4.... 3. Neurological Abnormalities (Lethargy, Tremors, Seizures, and Ataxia)

symptoms.name: Lethargy, depression, confusion, dilated pupils, ataxia (loss of coordination), muscle tremors, stupor, and seizures
symptoms.onset: 2–24 hours (typically follows gastrointestinal symptoms as the toxin is absorbed into the bloodstrea
symptoms.notes: Solanine inhibits the enzyme acetylcholinesterase in the brain and impairs mitochondrial function, severely disrupting the central nervous system3.... Cats are exceptionally sensitive to these toxins because their specific liver metabolism (lack of glucuronidation) slows detoxification, resulting in more severe and prolonged neurological effects than in dogs48. 4. Cardiac and Circulatory Disorders

symptoms.name: Abnormally slow heart rate (bradycardia) and circulatory failure
symptoms.notes: This occurs at higher toxic doses due to the severe overstimulation of the parasympathetic nervous system59. If left untreated, circulatory failure can lead to death4. 5. Hemolysis, Anemia, and Kidney Damage

symptoms.name: Hemolysis (destruction of red blood cells), resulting in anemia, jaundice (icterus), and hemoglobinuria (dark red or brown urine)
symptoms.notes: In severe cases, the toxins destroy the red blood cells4. The released hemoglobin from this destruction is nephrotoxic and can subsequently damage the kidneys, contributing to acute kidney failure3.... 6. Respiratory Paralysis

symptoms.name: Difficulty breathing progressing to respiratory paralysis
symptoms.onset: 2–24 hours (usually in the terminal stages of severe poisoning)
symptoms.notes: In particularly severe, untreated cases of nightshade poisoning, the ultimate cause of death is often respiratory paralysis and cardiovascular collapse
